solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    vector<long long> maximumEvenSplit(long long finalSum) {
        vector<long long> result;
        if (finalSum % 2 != 0) {
            return result;
        }
        
        long long k = find_max_k(finalSum);
        long long R = finalSum - k * (k + 1);
        
        if (R == 0) {
            for (int i = 1; i <= k; ++i) {
                result.push_back(2 * i);
            }
        } else {
            for (int i = 1; i < k; ++i) {
                result.push_back(2 * i);
            }
            result.push_back(2 * k + R);
        }
        
        return result;
    }
    
private:
    long long find_max_k(long long finalSum) {
        long long low = 1;
        long long high = 2 * sqrt(finalSum) + 1; // Estimate upper bound
        long long best_k = 0;
        
        while (low <= high) {
            long long mid = low + (high - low) / 2;
            long long s = mid * (mid + 1);
            
            if (s <= finalSum) {
                best_k = mid;
                low = mid + 1;
            } else {
                high = mid - 1;
            }
        }
        
        return best_k;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(k)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently finds the maximum number of unique even integers by leveraging the sum of the first k even numbers. Using binary search, it quickly determines the largest k such that the sum of the first k even numbers does not exceed the target sum. The remainder is then distributed to form the largest possible set of unique even integers. This approach ensures optimal performance and minimal energy usage.
